items.mistake: Saving important outputs only to the VM's local disk.
items.fix: Mount Google Drive (or download) and write results there.
items.why: The local filesystem is wiped when the runtime disconnects.
items.example: `open('/content/results.csv', 'w')` → gone in a few hours. `open('/content/drive/MyDrive/results.csv', 'w')` (after `drive.mount(...)`) → persists across sessions.

items.mistake: Hard-coding API keys in shared Colab cells.
items.why: Keys saved in the notebook leak to anyone you share it with.
items.example:
```python
# BAD: visible to every collaborator
openai_key = 'sk-abc123...'

# GOOD: secret stays in your account
from google.colab import userdata
openai_key = userdata.get('OPENAI_API_KEY')
```

items.mistake: Expecting a long training job to run unattended for hours.
items.fix: Checkpoint to Drive and design for idle timeouts/disconnects.
items.why: Free runtimes have time limits and can disconnect.
items.example:
```python
if epoch % 5 == 0:
    torch.save(model.state_dict(), f'/content/drive/MyDrive/ckpt/epoch_{epoch}.pt')
```
A disconnect at hour 6 loses at most 5 epochs, not the whole run.

items.mistake: Assuming a GPU is always attached.
items.fix: Select GPU in Runtime settings and verify with nvidia-smi.
items.why: The default runtime is CPU; accelerators must be chosen and may be unavailable.
items.example: First cell: `!nvidia-smi || echo 'NO GPU'`. If you see 'NO GPU', go to Runtime → Change runtime type → T4 GPU before training — otherwise the training silently runs on CPU and takes 50× longer.
